title: Tabernaemontana amblyocarpa
intro: Tabernaemontana amblyocarpa, also known as the West African yellow bush, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of T. amblyocarpa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the latter being one of the most common herbal preparations made from T. amblyocarpa.
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana amblyocarpa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Tabernaemontana, which comprises about 100 species of tropical plants. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. The classification of Tabernaemontana amblyocarpa is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships among different plant species.
